Name: -1_conversations_provide_proud_speak
Representative_Docs:
['October is Emotional Wellness Month! 💙 Your mental health matters, and Maryland Health Connection offers plans that cover essential mental health services like therapy, counseling, and more. Take care of your mind and body—enroll today for the support you deserve!', 'As Mental Health Awareness Month wraps up, LAFC reminds everyone that mental health matters every day. Thank you to our partners at the Los Angeles County Department of Mental Health for encouraging all of us to reach out and seek help when we need it.

Wellness, connection and
you or someone you know needs mental health support or resources, visit
dmh.lacounty.gov
.', 'World Mental Health Day was observed on October 10, 2024. On this day, the Union Health Ministry celebrated the success of the National Tele Mental Health Programme, Tele MANAS, which has completed two years and handled over 1.45 million calls. The Tele MANAS toll-free helpline,
-800-891-4416, offers multilingual support in 20 languages and has been instrumental in connecting individuals with mental health professionals.

At the commemoration event in New Delhi, Smt. Aradhana Patnaik, Additional Secretary of MoHFW and Managing Director of the National Health Mission (NHM, launched the Tele MANAS mobile app and video call facility.

IIIT-Bangalore is proud of its role in developing the Tele MANAS mobile app, which offers free and confidential 24/7 mental health support across India. Developed in-house with guidance from the Tele MANAS Apex Centre at NIMHANS, the app is available on the Google Play Store, with an iOS version coming soon. It includes features such as 24/7 support, an extensive knowledge base, and a user-friendly interface.

Video consultations represent a significant upgrade to the existing audio service, allowing mental health professionals to provide a more personalized experience. This service will initially launch in Karnataka, Jammu & Kashmir, and Tamil Nadu, with plans for nationwide expansion.
#IIITB
#IIITBangalore
#WorldMentalHealthDay
#MentalHealthAwareness
#TeleMANAS
#TeleHealth']

e information and to find events near you, visit
TakeActionLA.com
🔗", 'May is Mental Health Awareness Month: a time to pause, reflect, and remember that mental well-being matters every day, in every space. 

It’s also a time to break stigma, share resources, and reaffirm that conditions like anxiety, depression, and trauma deserve understanding,
silence.

At the Trevor Noah Foundation, we believe this is especially true in our schools. Learning can only truly happen when learners and teachers feel safe, supported, and emotionally grounded.

Through our partnership with @MyOnlineTherapist, our Khulani Schools Programme provides access to trained Care Agents who walk alongside learners and teachers through the everyday realities of school life, offering consistent, compassionate psychosocial support when it’s needed most.

Because mental health is not a moment, it is a commitment we carry throughout.
#MentalHealthAwarenessMonth
#MentalHealthMatters
#MOT
#KSP']

["We've produced a mental health ROI tool. It's based on evidence, can be adapted to local conditions and shows the economic benefits of each intervention. Read more:

#PublicHealthMatters", 'Everyone deserves access to mental health care. Black adults and youth can face additional systemic barriers to the care they need, including lack of local, culturally competent treatment options. If you need help, you don’t have to carry things alone.', 'This week, the Weather Events and Mental Health in Africa (WEMA) Project is hosting a Consortium Meeting in Naivasha, Kenya. Partners from the African Population and Health Research Center (
@
aphrc
), Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI), South Africa, Centre de Recherche en
Burkina Faso, and Comite para a Saude, Mozambique will review progress on research into the mental health impacts of floods, droughts, and heatwaves.

As climate extremes intensify, understanding their impact on mental health becomes increasingly urgent. Prof. Collins Iwuji (WEMA PI, AHRI) emphasized the importance of Africa-led collaboration, interdisciplinary research, and translating evidence into action through engagement with communities and policymakers.

APHRC’s
@
agnes_kiragga
highlighted ongoing work in Climate and Mental Health Research at the Center and the urgent need to address the intersection of climate change and mental health in Africa.
@
Wekesah
presented findings from the completed systematic review, which noted strong links between extreme weather events and mental health outcomes, including anxiety, depression, and post traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), particularly among vulnerable groups.

Country teams presented their latest findings, sharing progress and emerging evidence from research conducted in their study sites.

#GlobalHealth

Name: 11_suicide_crisis_prevention_talk
Representative_Docs:
["Recognizing the need for preventative mental health assistance programs and aid for deputies responding to mental health or substance abuse crisis calls,
@
SheriffStaly
established the Behavioral Response Unit last year. The BRU responds with deputies, assists in crisis
ven helped train K-9 handlers to quickly find missing endangered persons.

We're proud of our team of Mental Health Specialists, who have responded to over 500 calls for service and provided care coordination for over 60 individuals within the past year. Here is just a glimpse of the important work they do ⬇️

💚 Last fall, BRU Mental Health Specialist, Megan Ionadi, joined patrol units at the scene of a suicide threat in which a female had discharged a firearm inside a residence while she was extremely
intoxicated and threatening to commit suicide. A member of FCSO’s Crisis Negotiation Team (CNT) tried to make contact with the woman, but she said she was not willing to speak with Law
Enforcement. 

Instead, Ionadi, with assistance from the CNT member, was able to talk her out of the residence. The woman specifically requested to go to detox as she had relapsed with drinking alcohol. Once she exited the residence, deputies placed her in protective custody under the Baker Act.
💚 During another incident, BRU Mental Health Specialist, Teresa Shaw, assisted deputies at a trespassing call involving a schizophrenic man that was having an episode. Shaw was able to de-escalate the individual, enroll him in mental health services, and has since been following up with him. During initial contact, it was discovered the subject did not have insurance and was not receiving any psychiatric services. Today, he is now stable, insured, on medication, and has housing.
#MentalHealthAwarenessMonth
#MentalHealthAwarenessWeek
#MentalHealthMatters", 'If you or someone you know is struggling with their mental health or need someone to talk to, you are not alone. Resources are available on the NCDHHS Suicide Prevention website for social or family situations, depression, anxiety, panic attacks, thoughts of suicide, alcohol or
t need someone to talk to.

No matter what you’re going through, you’re not alone.
📞 Call, text, or chat 988 for free, 24/7 crisis support
🤝 Talk to someone who’s been there—call the Peer Warmline at 1-855-PEERS NC
🚐 Need in-person help? Mobile Crisis Teams and Crisis Centers are here across North Carolina

Resources:
ncdhhs.gov/crisis-services
ncdhhs.gov/about/departme…
chat.988lifeline.org', 'Mental health affects us all, and no one should feel alone in their struggles. This Mental Health Awareness Month, the City of Huntington Beach joins communities across the nation in recognizing the importance of mental wellness and support.

This year’s theme, “Mental Health Is
healing and support can often be found in our everyday lives, through our communities, relationships, cultures, routines, and the people around us.

According to the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration:
• 1 in 5 adults experiences a mental health condition each year
• 1 in 6 youth experience a major depressive episode
• Suicide remains one of the leading causes of death among young people ages 10–24

If you or someone you know is struggling, help is available. Local mental health resources are available through the Orange County Health Care Agency at
ocnavigator.org/m/ocn
or call 855-625-4657. 

And remember, the Suicide & Crisis Lifeline is available 24/7, just call or text 988 for immediate support.

Remember that mental health matters.']
